questions.scenario: Traffic reached 100 percent on the new checkout revision. What makes the next 30 minutes a watch window instead of passive waiting?

options.text: Named signals, an owner, decision times, and a rollback target.
A calendar invite with no metrics.

questions.explanation: A watch window is active verification with evidence and decision points.

questions.scenario: Which signals belong in a checkout watch window?

options.text: The color of the deployment badge.

questions.explanation: The useful signals connect to user impact and likely failure causes.

questions.scenario: A health endpoint is green, but Application Insights shows checkout failures rising. What should the team do?

options.text: Treat health as one layer and investigate the user-path evidence.
Ignore user failures because health is green.
Close the release because the runtime is alive.

questions.explanation: A health check can pass while the business path fails. Verification uses multiple layers.
